metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend, Hackney! I've found a range of exciting activities for your little one, from exploring animals at city farms to getting creative at museum workshops. With the weather looking good for outdoor adventures, there's something for everyone to enjoy.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner with diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed and engaging environment to foster a love of reading in young children.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes and Overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central
transport.tip: Check term times before heading out!

activities.title: Victoria Park Activities
activities.description: Full fortnight of free, daily activities including sports and pond dipping.
activities.whyItWorks: Variety of activities to keep a 3-year-old engaged, with space to run around.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes from Hackney or walk along the canal
transport.tip: Check the Victoria Park Facebook page for the schedule and booking details.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Museum designed for children with interactive displays, workshops, and an Open Studio.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed with young children in mind, offering hands-on experiences and creative play.

transport.tip: Check for special events and workshops on their website.

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends
activities.description: Explore how the iconic characters are brought to life.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging for young children due to the well-known characters and visual displays.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance online.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Step into historical homes through the ages with hands-on exhibits and a beautiful garden.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a sensory experience with historical homes and a lovely enclosed garden.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes or Overground to Hoxton
transport.tip: Pick up an activity sheet or explorer backpack from the front desk.

activities.title: East London City Farm Visits
activities.description: Visit local city farms to see spring arrivals and farm animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for toddlers to see animals up close and enjoy outdoor space.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes and Overground depending on the farm
transport.tip: Check opening times and locations of farms in East London.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: A relaxed and engaging environment to foster a love of reading in young children
activities.whyItWorks: With a reading corner with diverse book-pairing service, this will be a great visit for your children.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes and Overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central
transport.tip: Check term times before heading out!

activities.title: Sound Explorers at Young V&A
activities.description: Explore sounds at Young V&A
activities.whyItWorks: This workshop allows 3 year olds explore sounds and be creative.

transport.tip: Please check opening times.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, fascinating exhibits, and interactive displays.
activities.whyItWorks: The dinosaur exhibit is a guaranteed hit with young children.

transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Interactive exhibits and displays that explore science and technology.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging for curious minds with hands-on activities and space exploration exhibits.

transport.tip: Check the website for shows and special events.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Museum with anthropology, natural history, musical instruments, gardens, and animal walk.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of indoor and outdoor activities, including animals, gardens, and quirky exhibits.

transport.tip: Visit the Animal Walk and gardens for free fun.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world cultures and history with engaging exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers activity backpacks and trails to engage children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube (various lines) to Tottenham Court Road or Holborn
transport.tip: Visit the family desk for activity sheets and backpacks.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace and Gardens
activities.price: Entry from around the mid-teens, with under-5s free.
activities.description: Explore historic rooms, gardens, and the palace grounds while participating in fun activities for children
activities.whyItWorks: The Magic Garden playground is a great place for kids, with towers, slides and loads of space to run wild.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Waterloo, then take a train to Hampton Court
transport.tip: There is a Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt, roaming around the gardens and finding hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Hands-on maritime exhibits and a dedicated children's gallery.
activities.whyItWorks: The All Hands Children's Gallery is designed just for kids, and they have character encounters on Saturdays.

transport.tip: Check for family activities and workshops.

activities.title: Cutty Sark
activities.description: Easter Egg hunt over Easter weekend with Easter egg trail for the whole family!
activities.whyItWorks: Check Website

transport.tip: A fun filled family day!

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.price: Some paid activities and some free
activities.description: Southbank Centre Spring Family Fun – a festival of fun and creativity for families with amazing puppetry and lots of free fun in the Easter break.
activities.whyItWorks: This exciting venue has lots for young children to see, do and enjoy.

transport.fromHackney: Underground Stations Waterloo, Embankment Main Line Rail Stations Waterloo, Waterloo Eas, Charing Cross Buses stop on Waterloo Bridge, in York Road, in Belvedere Road and in Stamford Street.
transport.tip: The huge BrewDog Waterloo at The Sidings adjoining Waterloo Station is a family favourite with its ping pong tables, duckpin bowling lanes and the famous BrewDog slide.

activities.title: Polka Theatre
activities.description: Children's theatre with performances and workshops for young audiences.
activities.whyItWorks: Dedicated to children's theatre with age-appropriate shows and activities.

transport.tip: Check the website for show schedules and age recommendations.

activities.title: Unicorn Theatre - Your Toys
activities.description: Head down to Unicorn from 22nd March to 12th April for Your Toys, a heart-warming show aimed at 5-9s. Children are invited to bring along a toy to lend for the performance and watch it come to life on stage, plus there's 10 minutes of playtime at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: This interactive show will enable your child to become immersed into the performance, making it an unforgettable experience for all.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to London Bridge
transport.tip: The Unicorn Theatre is a children's theatre in the London Borough of Southwark.

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: Show, Heartwarming and hilarious adventure.
activities.whyItWorks: This show is based on a book by Julia Donaldson.

transport.tip: A show for all the family to enjoy!

activities.title: I Want My Hat Back Trilogy
activities.times: 4.45pm on Thur, and at 4.45pm on 6 May. All tickets £10 at 5.00pm on Fri
activities.description: Jon Klassen's beloved books come alive on stage.
activities.whyItWorks: This show will be one the whole family will enjoy.

transport.tip: See Family Workshops: I Want My Hat Back, 11-12 Apr

activities.title: Peppa Pig's egg-stra special Easter Afternoon Tea Bus Tour
activities.description: Every day this Easter a Peppa Pig themed afternoon tea will be run with special Easter travel cup and Easter Egg hunt activity on a London bus.
activities.whyItWorks: Peppa Pig is a favourite for many children, this will be an unforgettable experience for your child.

transport.tip: Read my full review of the Peppa Pig afternoon tea bus here.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers
activities.description: Follow the themed egg trail, climb and explore across indoor and outdoor play, and meet animals along the way.
activities.whyItWorks: There will be plenty for your child to enjoy at this Easter event.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance to avoid disappointment.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun day out for all the family, exploring animals and Easter events.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance to avoid disappointment.

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Bluey at Kew
activities.description: Bluey-themed interactive activities for the whole family, including a Play Zone with 3D installations and the chance to meet the Bluey character.
activities.whyItWorks: Bluey is a loved character for so many, make sure to book this in advance.

transport.tip: Book in advance!
